created_by: Wilderculture WILD Toolbox session (Claude) — priority-gap batch. Built from agency/scientific sources (JNCC SAC H6430, NVC U17, Averis upland vegetation). NO dedicated UKHab primary code and NO field sheet — both flagged. v3.3.

overlay_provenance.note: Two flags. (1) No source_level-7 practitioner field sheet exists; the wild_assessment is Claude-derived PROVISIONAL by analogy to the sheet-backed records (esp. g2b calcareous grassland, h1c5/h1c6 montane, f2c flushes — all of which already reference this tall-herb ledge flora). (2) UKHab v2.01 has NO dedicated primary code for tall-herb communities — see ukhab_identity.code_status. Identity/ecology rest on JNCC/NVC (high confidence); the overlay is moderate confidence, for review/replacement.

ukhab_identity.ukhab_level2_name: Grassland (loosely — see code_status; physically a montane ledge community)
ukhab_identity.composite_pk_note: Keyed on the ANNEX I code H6430 because UKHab provides no dedicated primary code (see code_status). Unique across the dataset. This is the one non-UKHab-keyed habitat record so far — a deliberate, flagged exception.

code_status.issue: STRUCTURAL FLAG: UKHab v2.01 has NO dedicated primary code for tall-herb communities. The Grassland Level-4 list runs g3a, g3b, g3c, g4 — no tall-herb letter.
code_status.how_ukhab_records_it: Per UKHab/metric guidance: the Annex I tall-herb community is named 'Grassland - Tall herb communities (H6430)' in the Statutory Metric (an Annex I type elevated in the metric), but in UKHab proper it is recorded against the HOST grassland (commonly g3c 'Other neutral grassland' for non-Annex tall-herb) and/or via the 'Tall forbs' SECONDARY code. Explicit guidance: 'Tall herb habitat that does not meet the Annex 1 definition should be recorded as Other neutral grassland (g3c).' The 'Tall forbs' secondary code is DISTINCT from the Annex I 'Tall herb communities (H6430)' — do not conflate.
code_status.recommendation: Treat like juniper: record on the host habitat (g3c, or the montane/calcareous grassland it sits in) with the H6430 Annex flag and/or Tall forbs secondary code. Verify against the full UKHab v2.01 workbook in case a primary code is added.

secondary_codes: Tall forbs (secondary code — DISTINCT from H6430; see code_status)
ukhab_identity.secondary_codes_notes: The 'Tall forbs' secondary code applies to tall-forb stands generally; it is NOT the Annex I tall-herb community. Use with care.
ukhab_identity.habitat_name: Tall herb communities (montane tall-herb ledge / hydrophilous tall-herb fringe)
ukhab_identity.parent_category: Grassland / montane ledge (no clean UKHab parent)
ukhab_identity.annex1_habitat: H6430 Hydrophilous tall herb fringe communities of plains and of the montane to alpine levels

sources.title: JNCC SAC habitat account — H6430 Tall herb communities (hydrophilous tall-herb fringe)
sources.specific_claim: H6430 = NVC U17 (Luzula sylvatica–Geum rivale); ungrazed base-rich upland cliff ledges; refuge for rare grazing-sensitive montane plants; one of the few near-natural habitats remaining in Britain.

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01 + Statutory Metric condition sheets
sources.specific_claim: No dedicated UKHab primary code for tall-herb communities; metric names 'Grassland - Tall herb communities (H6430)'; non-Annex tall-herb recorded as g3c; 'Tall forbs' is a distinct secondary code.

nvc_communities: U17 Luzula sylvatica–Geum rivale tall-herb community
cross_references.nvc_communities_notes: U17 is the defining community — a species-rich mix of tall broad-leaved herbs, most otherwise rare in the uplands because of their sensitivity to grazing.
cross_references.phase1_equivalent: Montane tall-herb ledge vegetation (no exact Phase 1 unit)
cross_references.wild_sheet_ref_note: No Wilderculture field sheet; but this flora is already referenced inside the g2b, h1c5, h1c6 and f2c records as the ungrazed ledge community.

sources.title: An Illustrated Guide to British Upland Vegetation (U17 tall-herb)
sources.specific_claim: U17 tall-herb ledge vegetation composition and ecology.

ecology.description: The ungrazed tall-herb vegetation of base-rich upland cliff ledges and steep inaccessible slopes — a species-rich mix of tall broad-leaved herbs (wood crane's-bill, water avens, globeflower, common valerian, meadowsweet, marsh hawk's-beard, great wood-rush, roseroot) that are otherwise rare in the uplands precisely because they are so sensitive to grazing. One of the few near-natural habitats remaining in Britain, it survives mainly where stock and deer cannot reach, and is a living indicator of what much richer upland vegetation could be under low grazing. Already referenced across the calcareous-grassland, montane and flush records as 'the tall-herb ledge flora'.
condition_indicators_good: A rich, tall, structurally diverse herb community on base-rich ledges/steep ground
The full grazing-sensitive specialist assemblage present
Ungrazed or very lightly grazed; tall structure intact
Often in intimate mosaic with other Annex I montane habitats
condition_indicators_poor: Browsed/grazed down — reduced to grasses with the tall herbs lost (the usual fate wherever reachable)
Confined to ever-smaller inaccessible ledge fragments
Shaded out or invaded; trampling damage
ecosystem_functions: A refuge and gene pool for rare, grazing-sensitive montane plants
A near-natural reference community — the benchmark for ungrazed upland vegetation
A distinct invertebrate and pollinator resource on the tall herbs
Part of intimate Annex I montane mosaics
ecology.whole_system_role: The grazing-relief indicator of the uplands — the flora that persists only out of reach of herbivores, and so the clearest sign of what reduced grazing could restore more widely.

successional_neighbours.habitat_code: g2b calcareous grassland / h1c5 montane heath / f2c base-rich flush
successional_neighbours.note: Forms intimate mosaics with these on base-rich montane ground; the records already cross-reference this ledge flora.
Where browsing is removed from base-rich ground, tall-herb can extend off the ledges — the WILD restoration signal.

sources.title: JNCC SAC H6430 account
sources.specific_claim: Ungrazed base-rich ledges; grazing-sensitive specialist herbs; near-natural refuge; intimate Annex I mosaics.

ukhab_condition.condition_grade_note: Assigned per parcel; the tall-herb specialist assemblage and ungrazed tall structure are the diagnostics.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Favourable
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Rich, tall U17 herb community on base-rich ground; full specialist assemblage; ungrazed structure intact; extending where ground allows.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable recovering
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Browsing reduced; tall herbs recovering/spreading from ledge refugia.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable no change
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Persisting on ledges but not spreading; some specialist loss.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable declining
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Browsed down to grasses; specialists lost where reachable.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Near-absent
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Reduced to tiny inaccessible fragments only.

sources.title: JNCC SAC H6430 account
sources.specific_claim: Specialist assemblage and ungrazed structure define favourable condition; grazing is the threat.

wild_assessment.overlay_status: PROVISIONAL — Claude-derived (no practitioner field sheet); review/replace before operational use.

abiotic_template.parent_material_geology: Base-rich rock — cliff ledges, steep slopes, often with soil seepage
abiotic_template.climate: Upland, cool, often montane; sheltered ledge situations
abiotic_template.altitude_range_m: Montane to alpine levels, on ledges and steep ground
abiotic_template.aspect_exposure: Ungrazed/inaccessible base-rich ledges and steep slopes; sheltered
abiotic_template.bioclimate_notes: Best stands on the bigger base-rich cliffs — e.g. the largest UK U17 stand on Torridonian sandstone at Beinn Bhan (Coire na Poite); Breadalbane, the Pennine cliffs, Snowdonia.

elemental_conditions.convention_note: CURRENT-STATE reading. PROVISIONAL — Claude-derived, no field sheet.

water.state: Base-rich ledges, often with soil seepage
water.descriptor: Sheltered, often seepage-moistened ledges (the 'hydrophilous' in the Annex name); spans drier to wetter forms.

earth.descriptor: Base-rich, fertile ledge soils — rich enough to carry tall, lush herb growth.

air.exposure_bioclimate: Sheltered ledge microclimate within an exposed montane setting
air.descriptor: Sheltered pockets that let tall herbs grow where the open hill cannot.

fire.descriptor: Tall, productive herb growth per unit area — but tiny in extent; high-quality near-natural production released from grazing.

successional_envelope.current_stage: Near-natural climax of ungrazed base-rich ledges — held by inaccessibility, not management
range_of_natural_variability: Spreading off ledges where grazing removed (the restoration direction)
successional_envelope.successional_pathway: Held as a climax by being out of reach of herbivores. Reduce grazing on the surrounding base-rich ground and tall-herb can extend beyond the ledges — the clearest demonstration of grazing relief in the uplands.

wild_assessment.energy_flow_role: A tiny but near-natural, high-quality herb community — the benchmark for ungrazed upland vegetation and a refuge for grazing-sensitive specialists.
wild_assessment.wild_zone_rationale: PROVISIONAL. Rare, near-natural, Annex I, irreplaceable refugia → Protect & Restore. Its wider significance is as the grazing-relief signal for the whole WILD map: reducing grazing on base-rich ground lets this flora spread off the ledges (a Regenerate→Wilder outcome), which is why it is referenced across the calcareous, montane and flush records.
wild_assessment.disturbance_regime: Grazing-defined by ABSENCE — it survives because ungrazed. Protect from browsing; the wider lever is reducing grazing on surrounding base-rich ground so it can extend.

wild_management_target.detail: Protect surviving ledge stands from any increase in browsing; where feasible, reduce grazing on adjacent base-rich ground so the tall-herb flora can spread off the ledges. Do not burn. Treat as a near-natural reference and refuge. Re-grade trigger applies.
integrity_indicators: extension beyond inaccessible ledges where grazing reduced

wild_assessment.ecological_value_notes: Among the highest — a rare, near-natural Annex I habitat and a refuge for grazing-sensitive montane specialists found almost nowhere else; effectively irreplaceable.

rarity_irreplaceability.notes: Rare, near-natural, confined to ungrazed base-rich ledges; irreplaceable refugia and gene pool.

diversity.notes: A species-rich tall-herb assemblage of otherwise-rare montane plants.

energy_flow_contribution.notes: Tiny in extent; value is as refuge and reference, not throughput.

naturalistic_lu_ha.value: Nil — an ungrazed habitat by definition; protect from browsing
naturalistic_lu_ha.basis_note: It exists because it is out of reach of herbivores; the management is non-grazing/grazing-reduction, not a stocking figure.

wild_assessment.mosaic_role_note: The grazing-relief refugium of base-rich montane mosaics — what the wider ground could be without heavy grazing.
wild_assessment.preferential_grazing_magnet_note: TRUE in principle — the tall herbs are highly palatable and grazing-sensitive, which is exactly why they survive ONLY where herbivores cannot reach. Its confinement to ledges is the proof.
process_indicators: spread off ledges where grazing reduced
wild_assessment.process_indicators_note: PROVISIONAL overlay. The clearest landscape indicator of grazing relief.

sources.title: JNCC SAC H6430 account
sources.specific_claim: Ungrazed, near-natural, grazing-sensitive refuge; Annex I mosaics.

sources.title: Claude-derived WILD overlay (no field sheet)
sources.specific_claim: Elemental dials/zone/value axes inferred by analogy to sheet-backed records — PROVISIONAL.

forage.overall_palatability_notes: The tall herbs are highly palatable and grazing-sensitive — which is the whole point: they survive only where herbivores cannot reach. NOT a forage resource; it is the ungrazed refuge, and its palatability is precisely why it is confined to ledges (the same logic as scrub and the calcareous-grassland tall herbs).

forage_yield_by_grade.notes: Not a forage/stocking habitat — an ungrazed refuge to protect, not graze.

conservation_lu_ha.value: Nil — protect from grazing/browsing; reduce grazing on adjacent ground so it can spread
conservation_lu_ha.basis_note: Survives because ungrazed; management is protection and grazing reduction on surrounding ground.

sources.title: JNCC SAC H6430 account
sources.specific_claim: Ungrazed/very lightly grazed; grazing is the threat.

forage.palatability_rank: Very High (grazing-sensitive) — an ungrazed refuge, not a resource
forage.nutritional_quality_note: Highly palatable tall herbs; no toxicity theme. Relevant only as the reason for grazing sensitivity.

optimal_grazing_animals.notes: Ungrazed by definition; the surrounding ground is where grazing reduction helps it spread.

unsuitable_animals.breed: Deer/sheep with access to the ledges
unsuitable_animals.reason: Browse out the grazing-sensitive tall herbs — the reason the habitat is confined to inaccessible ground.

timing_windows.preferred_season: Protect from grazing year-round; reduce grazing on adjacent base-rich ground
timing_windows.rationale: Allow the grazing-sensitive flora to persist and spread
timing_windows.avoid: Any increase in browsing access; burning
timing_windows.per_species_notes: Deer management on surrounding ground in Scotland; sheep elsewhere.

management.intensity_guidance: Protection, not grazing. The active lever is reducing grazing on the surrounding base-rich ground (links to the calcareous-grassland and montane records).
management.regulatory_note: Many stands are SSSI/SAC (H6430) and several tall-herb specialists are notable/protected — check designation and consents. Do not burn. NO field sheet and NO dedicated UKHab code — confirm practitioner detail with Wilderculture and verify coding against the full UKHab v2.01 workbook before operational use.
management.monitoring_protocol: Tall-herb specialist assemblage, vegetation height/structure, browsing impact, extent beyond ledges. Apply the re-grade trigger.

bng_unit_value.value_note: Very high distinctiveness — Annex I priority (H6430). Among the highest; irreplaceable refugia. (Recorded in the metric as 'Tall herb communities (H6430)'.)

sources.title: Statutory Biodiversity Metric
sources.specific_claim: Tall herb communities (H6430) very high distinctiveness; confirm exact label.

key_positive_indicators: Trollius europaeus (globeflower), Geranium sylvaticum (wood crane's-bill), Cirsium heterophyllum (melancholy thistle)
Crepis paludosa (marsh hawk's-crepis/hawk's-beard), Sedum rosea (roseroot), Caltha palustris (marsh marigold)
tall, ungrazed, structurally rich herb sward on base-rich ground
key_negative_indicators: browsed-down sward (tall herbs lost to grasses)
integrity_indicators_fauna: the broader near-natural montane invertebrate fauna of inaccessible ground
integrity_indicators_flora: the U17 tall-herb specialist assemblage itself IS the integrity indicator — a refuge of grazing-sensitive montane plants found almost nowhere else
target_species_composition: full U17 tall-herb assemblage, ungrazed and structurally rich
specialists spreading off the ledges where grazing reduced
species_list.note: Skeleton only — full records in Phase 2B. This flora is the integrity indicator cross-referenced in the g2b, h1c5, h1c6 and f2c records; the species joins should link there.

fields_fully_populated: identity & code_status (H6430/U17 verified; NO dedicated UKHab code — flagged like juniper)
the grazing-relief / near-natural-reference framing and cross-links to the built records
fields_partially_populated: wild_assessment — PROVISIONAL, Claude-derived (no field sheet; moderate confidence)
fields_pending_research: A Wilderculture field sheet (or a decision to keep tall-herb as a cross-cutting theme rather than a standalone record)
Confirm UKHab coding approach against the full v2.01 workbook (host-habitat g3c + H6430 flag, or Tall forbs secondary code)
data_completeness.overall_confidence: good (identity/ecology high; overlay provisional; coding flagged)
data_completeness.notes: The tall-herb ledge flora finally given its own record. TWO honest flags: no dedicated UKHab primary code (record on host g3c + H6430 Annex flag / Tall forbs secondary code — verify the workbook), and no practitioner field sheet (overlay provisional). H6430/U17 is a rare, near-natural, Annex I refuge for grazing-sensitive montane plants — the clearest single indicator of grazing relief, and already referenced across the calcareous (g2b), montane (h1c5/h1c6) and flush (f2c) records.
